name: Cloudflare
one_liner: Cloudflare provides CDN, DNS, security, edge compute (Workers) and developer platform services, all manageable via a broad public REST API.
access_notes: Anyone can create a free Cloudflare account and immediately generate scoped API tokens from the dashboard (My Profile > API Tokens) with no payment or approval. Working credentials are available on the free tier; paid plans only gate certain product features, not API access itself.
api_notes: Cloudflare's REST API (api.cloudflare.com/client/v4) covers 2500+ endpoints across DNS, zones, Workers, R2, KV, Pages, WAF/security, load balancing, analytics, account management, etc. Official OpenAPI spec and multiple SDKs (cloudflare-python, cloudflare-typescript, Go) plus the Wrangler CLI. Clearly BROAD.
mcp_notes: Cloudflare (the vendor) ships official MCP servers: the cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare GitHub repo and a catalog of managed remote MCP servers (docs, observability, DNS analytics, Workers bindings, and a Code Mode API server covering the full API). Connect via OAuth from MCP clients.
main_blocker: None significant. Free account yields working scoped API tokens instantly; the only friction is choosing appropriate token scopes for the target products.

evidence.claim: Cloudflare API uses API tokens (user and account/service tokens) created from the dashboard as the recommended auth method
Cloudflare ships official MCP servers including a catalog of managed remote servers and an API MCP server covering 2500+ endpoints
cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare is Cloudflare's official MCP server repository

open_questions: Exact free-tier rate limits on the client API and whether legacy Global API Key auth is being deprecated in favor of tokens.
